items.title: Here are the five big takeaways from Kevin Warsh's first meeting as Fed chairman
items.summary: The Federal Reserve and Chairman Kevin Warsh on Wednesday followed the script on interest rates closely.

items.title: Jeffrey Gundlach says Fed's Warsh is not going to be the 'easy money' chairman many hoped for
items.summary: Gundlach said Warsh's stance reduces the risk of overly accommodative monetary policy that could reignite inflation and push longer-term borrowing costs higher.

items.title: Defense contractors would be barred from buying back their stock in bill approved by Senate panel
items.summary: A bill the Senate Armed Services Committee approved would prevent defense contractors from buying back their stock without Pentagon approval.

items.title: Analysis: Chairman Kevin Warsh’s task forces are the key to understanding the new Fed
items.summary: The new leader of the Fed left interest rates alone but made clear how he plans to rewire the central bank.

items.title: Chairman Warsh abstains from giving rate forecast as several members signal a hike in 2026
items.summary: The median projection called for the federal funds rate to end 2026 at 3.8%, a quarter percentage point above the current target range.

items.title: 2-year Treasury yield rockets higher as many Fed officials signal possible hike this year
items.summary: U.S. Treasury yields rose on Wednesday after the Fed held interest rates steady during Kevin Warsh's first policy meeting as chairman.

items.title: Fed giữ nguyên lãi suất
items.summary: Cục Dự trữ Liên bang Mỹ (Fed) giữ nguyên lãi suất lần thứ 4 liên tiếp, trong phiên họp đầu tiên dưới thời tân Chủ tịch Kevin Warsh.

items.title: Chi-Hua Chien saw Facebook coming; now he says the real AI winners won’t be selling AI
items.summary: Chi-Hua Chien has spent more than two decades as a venture capitalist, but he thinks like a cultural anthropologist.

items.title: Roelof Botha joins SpaceX’s board of directors
items.summary: The former Sequoia Capital leader is filling an "existing vacancy" on SpaceX's board, days after the company went public in the largest IPO ever.

items.title: After unveiling ridiculously expensive AR glasses, Snap’s stock takes a dive
items.summary: Snap's long-awaited smart glasses debut hasn't exactly done wonders for the company's stock.

items.title: NEA’s Tiffany Luck says enterprises are still figuring out their AI ROI
items.summary: Tokenmaxxing was the hottest trend in Silicon Valley earlier this year, with CEOs encouraging employees to push AI usage as far as it would go. Then the bill came due. Uber reportedly blew through its annual AI budget in a few months, some companies cut Claude licenses for parts of their org, and Meta killed its internal leaderboard.  This tension between […]

items.title: FTC lawsuit reveals how subscription scam networks evade app store enforcement
items.summary: A new FTC lawsuit reveals how sophisticated subscription app operators can allegedly use shell companies and payment infrastructure to stay active on app stores despite mounting consumer complaints.

items.title: World leaders want American AI. They just don’t want America to be able to turn it off.
items.summary: French President Macron and Indian PM Modi raised alarms at the G7 summit that the U.S. could cut off access to American AI overnight  — a fear the Anthropic blackout just made real.

items.title: Anthropic becomes first AI startup to join the Frontier carbon removal coalition
items.summary: Anthropic has joined the Frontier coalition, which received another $915M in pledges to fund carbon removal projects.

items.title: Cybercriminals allegedly hacked tens of thousands of Fortinet firewalls used by major companies all over the world
items.summary: An alleged Russian-speaking group of cybercriminals is reportedly compromising and targeting several major companies that use Fortinet Firewalls and VPNs through previously known passwords.

items.title: Social media’s next evolution: user-controlled algorithms
items.summary: Social media feeds are becoming more customizable as platforms like Threads, Instagram, and TikTok introduce tools that let users directly influence the algorithms powering their recommendations.

items.title: Here’s our monthly update on all 35 portfolio stocks, including Cramer's favorite name to buy
items.summary: Jim Cramer and Jeff Marks covered their latest thinking on all of the Investing Club's stocks during the June Monthly Meeting.
